page: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an ARM-based Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 3.4 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is not utilized. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage options for disk operations.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The D32ps_v6 achieves top-tier ratings in memory bandwidth, with read-only large block speeds of 450.97 GB/sec and write speeds of 188.48 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by strong scores in stress-ng multi-core testing, bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with Clang. Database operations also perform exceptionally well, yielding top-tier results in PassMark database benchmarks and strong throughput in Redis SET operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and excellent memory throughput per unit of compute, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 instances for compatible workloads. However, certain hardware tradeoffs exist. The absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets limits its suitability for specialized vector math, and the lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage attachments. Furthermore, while small and medium LLM inference tasks run at average speeds, large-scale LLM inference exhibits poor prompt processing performance.

This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database management, and memory-intensive data processing. It is also appropriate for Redis caching layers and general application hosting where ARM compatibility is established.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v6 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine configured with 32 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing and software compilation capabilities. However, the ARM64 architecture lacks AVX instruction sets, and large-scale LLM inference performance is limited. Qualitatively, the D32ps_v6 offers high resource density and memory efficiency for general-purpose workloads. It is highly suited for web serving, database management, Redis caching, and multi-threaded application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v6 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v6 is an ARM-based general-purpose VM with 32 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and dedicated multi-core performance for database and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.4 GHz ARM-based Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
Lacks local storage and onboard GPUs, utilizing remote Premium Storage instead.
Strong multi-core efficiency for software compilation and Redis caching.
Best suited for web serving, database management, and ARM-native application hosting.
